page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c5.xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8163 processor based on the x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The instance is configured with 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs, meaning hyperthreading is configured with 1 thread per core. It includes 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or GPU hardware is bundled with this instance type, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place the ecs.c5.xlarge in the lower tiers of modern cloud compute resources. In stress-ng and Geekbench tests, both single-core and multi-core CPU performance fall into the bottom 25% category. Memory bandwidth tests show weak to poor results, with uncached large-block read operations measuring 20.58 GB/sec and write operations at 18.68 GB/sec. Cryptographic and compression tasks also reflect these limitations, with single-threaded gzip compression registering at 19.02 GB/sec. For database and key-value operations, the instance achieves 2.86 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and a static web serving throughput of 3.36 GB/sec.

The primary tradeoff of the ecs.c5.xlarge is its modest processing and memory throughput relative to newer hardware generations, which is reflected in its lower-tier benchmark rankings. However, its dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent performance without the CPU credit limitations of burstable instances. This makes it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for workloads that require dedicated compute resources but do not demand high memory bandwidth or intensive processing power. Suitable workloads include lightweight web serving, basic application hosting, small-scale database operations, and low-intensity background processing tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c5.xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor at 2.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and KVM hypervisor, this instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates modest performance, with CPU and memory bandwidth results generally falling into the bottom 25% of tested servers. The instance is suited for workloads requiring dedicated CPU allocation without the high resource demands of enterprise databases, such as lightweight web serving, basic application hosting, and small-scale data processing tasks.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c5.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and 2 Gbps network bandwidth, suitable for lightweight web serving and basic application hosting.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c5.xlarge features 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and 2 Gbps network bandwidth for lightweight dedicated workloads.
tagline: A dedicated compute instance with a balanced resource profile for lightweight application hosting and basic web serving workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8163 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and physical cores.
8.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core.
Baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps with no local storage.
Consistent performance via dedicated CPU allocation without burstable credits.
Best suited for lightweight web serving, basic hosting, and small databases.
